friction_items.description: Semantic routing is unstable on engineering-vocabulary benchmark prompts and produces high-confidence WRONG matches. With the full prompt ('benchmark three sorting algorithms at five sizes, 10 runs each, log-log scaling, paired Wilcoxon, governor pinning') the router returned methodology/missing_data_strategy at semantic confidence=high (score 0.689). Shorter phrasings landed on the right protocol (method_comparison at 0.876). matched_triggers was empty — the trigger-fallback layer never fired because semantic confidence cleared the high threshold. From the maintainer's lens the issue is structural: (a) the engineering pack still contributes no algorithmic_benchmark protocol of its own so the only legitimate home for this ask is method_comparison via its addendum-as-step, and (b) trigger-based regex routing is being silenced by overconfident semantic matches. The baseline's #1 recommendation — add a first-class engineering/test/algorithmic_benchmark protocol — was not acted on in Phase 9 or Phase 11; that gap is now the single biggest remaining maintainer concern.

Still no first-class benchmark harness tool. The engineering pack ships exactly 3 tools (tool_engineering_fmea_render, tool_engineering_fault_tree_render, tool_engineering_requirements_matrix), all unrelated to algorithmic benchmarking. A 3-algorithm × 5-size × 10-run sweep is still 150 invocations that the AI has to orchestrate by hand in a Python script. No tool_engineering_benchmark_harness, no tool_engineering_scaling_fit, no tool_replicate_runs primitive was added in Phase 9. Combined with the routing miss above, this means even when the AI lands on the right protocol there is no native tool to execute the protocol's core ask.

sys_active_tools('methodology/method_comparison') returns 13 essentials-only tools (sys_*, mem_log, tool_route, tool_plan). None of the 5 tools the protocol body actually references (tool_preregister, tool_research_method, tool_search, tool_engineering_requirements_matrix, tool_route) are in the active set. The tool_def claims the call returns 'essentials + everything the protocol's decomposition actually calls' — that contract is not being honoured for method_comparison. Maintainer impact: the AI either burns a turn calling sys_protocol_get format='full' to discover real tools, or it muddles forward with the essentials set and never reaches the execution tools the protocol assumes.

The _err envelope still ships {status:'error', error: <string>} with no error_code, no suggested_action, no docs_link. Baseline gap #4 unaddressed. Concrete symptom from this run: tool_audit with valid (scope, dimension) but no workspace returned 'workspace/ not found' as a string, wrapped inside data.{status:error} inside a top-level {status:success} envelope — a double-envelope shape that confuses both AI clients and maintainers grepping logs. The good news: bad-dimension errors do now include a docs path ('See docs/V2_MIGRATION_TABLE.md for valid combinations.'), which is a partial improvement worth noting.

sys_active_tools' description still claims 'triaging all 212 tools per turn' — but the actual TOOL_DEFINITIONS surface is 146. The Phase 9 consolidation got the structural reduction (36 audit tools → 9; dashboard 7 → 1; step cluster 8 → 2) but did not sweep its own doc strings. Other drift: load_protocol() function docstring still says "format: 'full' (default)" even though the schema layer now defaults to 'summary'. Maintainer concern: anyone navigating the codebase via grep on '212' or 'default=full' gets a misleading mental model.

engineering_or_systems_benchmark_addendum is STILL step 10 of 11 inside methodology/method_comparison (only change since baseline: protocol_completion was appended as step 11). The baseline flagged this as an anti-pattern — the engineering doctrine is buried inside an ML scaffold. With scope_tags now present on every protocol (workflow_shape: [systems_benchmark] is on method_comparison itself) the addendum step is doubly redundant: the tag already exposes the intent without needing the step. The cheapest fix path post-2.0 is: lift the addendum body into a new engineering/test/algorithmic_benchmark protocol, cross-link from method_comparison's description, and let scope_tags drive the routing precedence.

Baseline gap #6 unaddressed: no protocol in the engineering pack declares a telemetry: block, none call tool_reliability_log_event (which now lives under the consolidated tool_reliability dispatcher). tool_reliability_report still has nothing to aggregate for engineering work. Low rather than medium because the consolidation itself was the bigger structural win — but the data gap remains.

Baseline gap #10 unaddressed: tool_audit_quality_full still returns the legacy aggregator dict + an audit_master_v2 reference but no top_blocker field and no one-line remediation hint. A maintainer triaging a CI failure still has to inspect a large JSON to find the highest-severity violation.

Baseline gap #5 unaddressed: tool_audit_reproducibility (now reached via tool_audit dimension='reproducibility') still has no audit_budget_s or sample_strategy parameter. For a 150-run benchmark this still implies a 75+ minute audit. The consolidation made the surface smaller without making the underlying cost model smarter.

convoluted_tools: tool_audit (still a 21-dimension dispatcher; the dimension enum is long and the (scope, dimension) matrix is documented only in the migration table)

tool_reliability (the lessons+failure+reliability 10→2 collapse left a single dispatcher with operation= now covering log_event, report, failure_record, failure_check, failure_list, dead_end_lessons, mistake_replay — readable but heavy)
confusing_protocols: methodology/method_comparison (engineering benchmark addendum still buried as step 10 of 11; scope_tags now redundant with the addendum step)
engineering/test/build_test_fix_loop vs engineering/test/test_failure_causation (baseline precedence concern unresolved; both still live and overlap on test-failure triggers)
improvements_vs_baseline: Tool-surface consolidation: TOOL_DEFINITIONS dropped from 226 to 146 (-35%); the audit category alone collapsed from 36 to 9 (-75%), and a unified tool_audit(scope, dimension) dispatcher now backs the deprecated audit aliases via param injection.

22 of the 32 baseline never-called tools (~69%) are gone in v2 — the dashboard cluster (7 → 1), the reviewer/rebuttal cluster (5 tools removed), qualitative pack pruned (3 tools removed), wet-lab pack pruned (3), theory_math pruned (3).

Every tool def now carries status: 'live' (146/146) and pack: <name> fields — the maintainer-grep story is real. The pack split is clean (core: 123, engineering/humanities/qualitative/theory_math/wet_lab: 3 each, 4 small adapters).
Every protocol carries scope_tags {domain, audience, workflow_shape} (43/43 methodology + 7/7 engineering). method_comparison correctly carries workflow_shape: [systems_benchmark].

MCP server now ships an instructions field on handshake that names the boot sequence (sys_boot → tool_route → sys_protocol_get format=summary → sys_active_tools). Removes the prior turn-1 discovery friction for compliant clients.
sys_protocol_get default format flipped from 'full' to 'summary' at the schema layer. Verified payload: ~3.5K chars / ~875 tokens (down from baseline-claimed 1.5–3K full).
tool_route response now carries a recommended_action string telling the AI exactly which next tool + args to call. Removes a class of turn-2 routing-deliberation friction.
tool_semantic_route mirrors the recommended_action contract (Phase 9 cross-cutting), so the fallback semantic path no longer leaves the AI guessing.

Phase 10 server.py refactor delivered: the historical 7,499-line server.py is gone. The replacement is a 1,640-line src/research_os/server/ package with handlers/, tool_definitions/, envelopes.py, aliases.py, dispatch.py, entry.py. Tool defs split across 7 thematic files (~2,135 LoC total). Code navigation friction for a new maintainer is materially lower.

Bad-dimension errors on tool_audit now include a docs path ('See docs/V2_MIGRATION_TABLE.md for valid combinations.') — a partial step toward the suggested_action field even though the envelope schema itself is unchanged.

Deprecated aliases tracked in a single _DEPRECATED_ALIASES set in aliases.py with a deprecations.log under .os_state/ — the maintainer can audit alias usage before the next major hard-removes them, which is the right model.
remaining_recommendations: BLOCKER for v2.1: add engineering/test/algorithmic_benchmark protocol + tool_engineering_benchmark_harness primitive. The baseline's #1 recommendation is also v2's #1 maintainer concern. Routing the most common engineering ask through an ML protocol's step-10 addendum is the highest-friction structural debt in the v2 release.

BLOCKER for v2.1: extend the _error envelope to {status:'error', error_code: <snake_case>, error: <message>, suggested_action: <next>, docs_link: <url>}. Grepability by error_code is a hard requirement once external users start filing bug reports. The Phase 9 docs-path hint on tool_audit shows the team agrees in spirit; promote it to a schema-level contract.

Fix sys_active_tools(method_comparison): currently returns essentials only, not the 5 tools the protocol body actually references. Likely a regression in active-tools wiring during the consolidation — verify against the protocol's decomposition extractor.

Sweep documentation drift: 'all 212 tools' in sys_active_tools description → '146'; load_protocol docstring 'format: full (default)' → 'summary'. Add a CI check that the tool count in human-facing strings matches len(TOOL_DEFINITIONS).

Once algorithmic_benchmark protocol exists, delete the engineering_or_systems_benchmark_addendum step from method_comparison.yaml and replace it with a one-line cross-link in description. The scope_tags layer makes the addendum-as-step doubly redundant.

Tune semantic routing thresholds for engineering vocabulary: the 'benchmark algorithms with wall-clock and peak RSS' prompt landed on missing_data_strategy at score 0.689 with confidence=high. Consider per-domain calibration or letting the trigger layer veto a semantic high-confidence match when the matched protocol's scope_tags.workflow_shape is incompatible with engineering vocabulary in the prompt.
Add reproducibility_audit_budget_s + sample_strategy to tool_audit(dimension='reproducibility'). The 150-run benchmark still implies a >75-minute audit by default.

Add a 'telemetry:' block to each engineering protocol naming the event_types the AI should flush via tool_reliability(operation='log_event'). Without this, tool_reliability(operation='report') has nothing to aggregate for engineering work.
Add top_blocker + remediation_hint fields to tool_audit_quality_full output. Single biggest UX win for the synthesis-side maintainer flow.

Document the routing precedence between engineering/test/build_test_fix_loop and engineering/test/test_failure_causation explicitly in the engineering pack README — or merge into a single test_loop protocol with branching steps.

Continue pruning never-called tools: 10 baseline tools are still live and uncalled by any protocol in this scenario. Confirm whether mem_intake_regenerate / mem_citations_generate / writing_discussion_from_verdicts have legitimate callers in OTHER scenarios; if not, hard-remove in v2.1.

Flatten the double-envelope pattern: top-level {status:success, data:{status:error, message:…}} should resolve to {status:error, error_code:…, error:…} at the dispatcher boundary so log greps don't have to drill two levels.
